display.purpose: Wordmark and boot title — engraved, glowing, letter-spaced caps.

headline.purpose: The eulogy subject name — loudest word on screen.

title.purpose: A studio name carved on its headstone.

label.purpose: Uppercase tracked chrome — dates, counts, R·I·P.

shadows.name: stone-slab
shadows.purpose: A headstone at rest — the four-part molded-resin recipe.

shadows.name: stone-lifted
shadows.purpose: A stone raised toward the cursor — deeper drop, brighter glow.

shadows.name: modal-float
shadows.purpose: The eulogy lifted high above a blurred scrim.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Default strong ease-out for entrances, hover lifts, presses.

motion.name: ease-in-out
motion.purpose: On-screen movement that accelerates and decelerates.

motion.name: modal-out
motion.purpose: Eulogy exit — faster than entrance, asymmetric by design.

motion.name: grave-stagger
motion.purpose: Tombstone field rises in cascade on load.

components.name: Gravestone
components.description: Signature component — a rounded-top jewel-plastic headstone you click to open the eulogy.
components.css: .ds-grave { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 10px; width: 200px; padding: 26px 16px 20px; cursor: pointer; text-align: center; border: none; border-top: 1px solid rgba(196,250,130,.5); border-radius: 108px 108px 12px 12px; background: linear-gradient(158deg, rgba(158,228,86,.32), rgba(30,74,14,.5)); box-shadow: inset 0 2px 0 rgba(230,255,170,.4), inset 0 -34px 50px rgba(0,16,0,.5), 0 18px 30px rgba(0,0,0,.45), 0 0 36px rgba(124,210,58,.12); backdrop-filter: blur(8px); transition: transform 220ms cubic-bezier(0.23,1,0.32,1), box-shadow 220ms cubic-bezier(0.23,1,0.32,1); } .ds-grave-rip { font: 700 11px/1 'Chakra Petch',sans-serif; letter-spacing: .32em; color: rgba(20,46,8,.55); } .ds-grave-name { font: 700 18.5px/1.16 'Cinzel',serif; color: #0f2607; text-shadow: 0 1px 0 rgba(214,255,150,.55); } .ds-grave-dates { font: 600 12.5px 'Chakra Petch',sans-serif; color: rgba(18,44,8,.72); } .ds-grave-span { padding: 3px 10px; border: 1px solid rgba(20,46,8,.28); border-radius: 999px; background: rgba(15,38,7,.22); font: 700 10.5px 'Chakra Petch',sans-serif; letter-spacing: .18em; text-transform: uppercase; color: rgba(18,44,8,.82); } .ds-grave:hover { transform: translateY(-7px); box-shadow: inset 0 2px 0 rgba(230,255,170,.55), inset 0 -34px 50px rgba(0,16,0,.4), 0 24px 40px rgba(0,0,0,.5), 0 0 50px rgba(143,209,58,.4); } .ds-grave:active { transform: scale(0.97); }

components.name: Game Chip
components.description: Notable-game tag inside the eulogy — jewel-lime pill with a toxic border.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-block; padding: 7px 14px; border: 1px solid rgba(176,240,110,.3); border-radius: 999px; background: linear-gradient(160deg, rgba(150,225,80,.22), rgba(36,82,18,.4)); box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(230,255,170,.3); font: 400 13px 'Chakra Petch',sans-serif; letter-spacing: .02em; color: #eaffc0; }

components.name: Rating Badge
components.description: Toxic-lime IGDB score badge — one of the few elements allowed to glow.
components.css: .ds-rating { display: inline-block; padding: 2px 7px; border-radius: 999px; background: rgba(191,240,90,.92); box-shadow: 0 2px 6px rgba(0,0,0,.45); font: 700 10.5px 'Chakra Petch',sans-serif; letter-spacing: .04em; color: #0f2607; }

components.name: IGDB Cover Card
components.description: Cover tile in the eulogy's IGDB grid — art, rating badge, title, year.
components.html: ★ 88Hi-Fi Rush2023
components.css: .ds-igdb { display: block; width: 120px; text-decoration: none; color: inherit; transition: transform 200ms cubic-bezier(0.23,1,0.32,1); } .ds-igdb-cover { position: relative; display: block; aspect-ratio: 3/4; border: 1px solid rgba(176,240,110,.3); border-radius: 10px; background: linear-gradient(160deg, rgba(150,225,80,.18), rgba(24,60,10,.5)); box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(230,255,170,.25), 0 8px 20px rgba(0,0,0,.4); } .ds-igdb-rating { position: absolute; top: 6px; right: 6px; padding: 2px 7px; border-radius: 999px; background: rgba(191,240,90,.92); font: 700 10.5px 'Chakra Petch',sans-serif; color: #0f2607; } .ds-igdb-name { display: block; margin-top: 8px; font: 700 13px/1.2 'Cinzel',serif; color: #eaffc0; } .ds-igdb-year { display: block; margin-top: 2px; font: 600 11px 'Chakra Petch',sans-serif; letter-spacing: .08em; color: rgba(174,219,124,.7); } .ds-igdb:hover { transform: translateY(-3px); } .ds-igdb:hover .ds-igdb-cover { border-color: rgba(191,240,90,.6); box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(230,255,170,.35), 0 0 26px rgba(143,209,58,.35); } .ds-igdb:active { transform: scale(0.98); }

components.name: Eulogy Close Button
components.description: Small square ghost button in the modal header.
components.css: .ds-close { width: 34px; height: 34px; cursor: pointer; border: 1px solid rgba(176,240,110,.34); border-radius: 9px; background: rgba(150,225,80,.16); color: #dff7a8; font: 16px/1 'Chakra Petch',sans-serif; transition: background 200ms ease, transform 140ms cubic-bezier(0.23,1,0.32,1); } .ds-close:hover { background: rgba(170,245,95,.32); } .ds-close:active { transform: scale(0.92); }

narrative.overview: Xbox Cemetery is the translucent green plastic shell of an original Xbox, hollowed out and turned into a reliquary. The whole surface behaves like backlit jewel plastic, and the things it holds are the dead: studios shut down by the green machine, each laid out as a headstone you can pick up and read. The system runs on a single tension: carved stone (Cinzel) against early-digital chrome (Chakra Petch). It rejects the official Xbox site, generic gamer RGB, and the sterile wiki table.
keyCharacteristics: Translucent jewel-green plastic on every surface (layered greens, inset bevels, backdrop blur, glossy highlight).
Engraved-stone display type (Cinzel) against squared digital chrome (Chakra Petch).
Era hardware cues: green boot-orb, CRT scanlines + grain + vignette, the X-orb mark.
Reverent, dry, bittersweet — a wake, never a roast.
The atmosphere degrades gracefully: motion and effects pull back without breaking layout or meaning.

rules.name: The One Glow Rule
rules.body: Toxic Lime (#bff05a) is the only color allowed to glow, on at most one or two elements per view. Scarcity makes the glow read as sacred.

rules.name: The Stone-Ink Contract
rules.body: Stone Ink (#0f2607) is used only on a lit jewel-lime face, always with a light engraving text-shadow. Never on the void or dark green.

rules.name: The Two-Voice Rule
rules.body: Cinzel for the remembered (names, wordmark, epitaphs); Chakra Petch for the operational (labels, dates, counts). Never cross them.

rules.name: The Carved-Caps Rule
rules.body: Display and label type is uppercase with positive tracking. Lowercase Cinzel headings are prohibited.

rules.name: The Four-Part Slab Rule
rules.body: Every raised surface carries top highlight + inner bottom shadow + drop shadow + ambient glow. Drop one and the plastic looks painted-on.

dos: Do mold every raised surface as translucent jewel plastic — the four-part slab shadow plus backdrop blur. The glass is the brand.
Do keep the Two-Voice split absolute: Cinzel for the remembered, Chakra Petch for the operational.
Do reserve Toxic Lime glow for at most one or two elements per view.
Do pair Stone Ink only with a lit jewel-lime face and a light engraving text-shadow.
Do let the atmosphere degrade gracefully — every effect needs a prefers-reduced-motion fallback.
Do keep the humor dry and carved-in — bittersweet epitaphs, never punchlines.
donts: Don't make it look like the official Xbox/Microsoft site — no corporate gloss, no marketing cheer.
Don't drift into generic gamer RGB — no neon rainbow, no angular esports tropes.
Don't let it become a sterile wiki or data table — the mood carries equal weight to the facts.
Don't go goofy, meme-y, or clickbait — it's a wake, not a roast.
Don't put Stone Ink on the void or dark green, and don't set Cinzel headings lowercase.
Don't spread the Toxic Lime glow across many elements — scarcity is what makes it sacred.
